philippines.title: NBA: Jokic fined $50,000, Randle $35K for Nuggets-Wolves spat
philippines.description:
NEW YORK — Denver’s Nikola Jokic was fined $50,000 and Minnesota’s Julius Randle was fined $35,000 for their roles in an altercation near the end of Game 4 of the teams’ playoff series, the NBA announced Sunday. Both will be eligible to play when the series resumes Monday with Game 5 in Denver. READ: NBA: Dosunmu helps Timberwolves overcome injuries, Nuggets for 3-1 lead The incident was evidently sparked when Minnesota’s Jaden McDaniels took an uncontested layup with 2.1 seconds left and the Timberwolves already leading by 14 — a play the Nuggets took exception to, given how the game […]...
Keep on reading: NBA: Jokic fined $50,000, Randle $35K for Nuggets-Wolves spat

philippines.title: BSKE 'difficult' to stage in 2027, Comelec says
philippines.description:
MANILA – The Commission on Elections (Comelec) on Monday said holding the Barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan Elections (BSKE) next year would be challenging. “It looks like it will be difficult,” Comelec Chairperson George Garcia said in a Viber message to reporters. He did not elaborate. Garcia earlier said the poll body prefers to hold the BSKE this year to allow sufficient time to prepare for the May 2028 National and Local Elections. READ: Comelec to ask for additional P3 billion if BSKE delay pushes through The BSKE is currently scheduled for Nov. 2, but there have been calls to postpone […]...
Keep on reading: BSKE 'difficult' to stage in 2027, Comelec says

philippines.title: BI: 2 Americans killed in Negros clash arrived in PH this year
philippines.description:
MANILA – Two Americans reportedly among those killed in last week’s deadly clash between government troops and suspected New People’s Army rebels in Toboso, Negros Occidental arrived in the Philippines only this year, the Bureau of Immigration (BI) said Monday. BI spokesperson Dana Sandoval declined to provide further details on the travel history of American nationals Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem. “We cannot disclose at this point,” she said in an interview. “Both of them only arrived this year,” she added. READ: Negros encounter: 2 Americans among suspected rebels killed Sandoval noted that the two did not enter the […]...
Keep on reading: BI: 2 Americans killed in Negros clash arrived in PH this year

energy.description: It has been said by many from the very start of the U.S.’s ‘Epic Fury’ war against Iran that no clear endgame was in sight whereby U.S. President Donald Trump could achieve his stated aims at the outset of the conflict. These were to change regime, permanently nullify Iran’s nuclear threat, destroy the country’s ballistic missile capabilities, and halt its support for terrorist proxies. It has also been said by many that Washington unaccountably failed to anticipate Iran’s blockade of the vital global transit…

energy.title: Japan's Top Utility JERA Has Secured LNG Supply Through July
energy.description: Japan's biggest LNG importer and largest power producer, JERA, has secured its LNG supply through July and will adjust its procurement strategy to be more flexible amid the Middle East conflict that has trapped LNG supply behind the Strait of Hormuz. “At present, we have sufficient stock to last until July,” Masato Otaki, JERA's Executive Officer and Head of the Financial Strategy and Planning Division, said on Monday, as carried by Reuters. “Depending on how long the blockade continues, we will need to adjust our procurement…
